title: quercus rotundifolia var. brevicupulata
intro: Quercus rotundifolia var. brevicupulata, also known as the holm oak, is a variety of the deciduous evergreen Quercus rotundifolia tree, commonly found in the Iberian Peninsula and North Africa. The leaves of this plant are used in herbal medicine, and are known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems and respiratory infections. The plant's leaves have been traditionally used to treat ailments like bronchitis, coughs, and fever, and are also believed to have wound-healing properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support the immune system and promote overall well-being.
